---
title: "Small Area Estimation of Poverty and Nutrition in Bolivia"
pagetitle: "Poverty & Nutrition in Bolivia"
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}
# Poverty & Nutrition in Bolivia
::: {.subtitle}
Small Area Estimation at the Municipal Level — 2024
:::
::: {.lead}
High-resolution poverty and malnutrition maps for all 339 municipalities of Bolivia,
combining household survey data with census information through a three-layer
statistical framework: Distributional Random Forests, Bayesian spatial smoothing, and Optimal Transport calibration.
:::
:::

## Key Findings

::: {.stats-grid}
::: {.stat-card}
[37.8%]{.stat-value}
[National Poverty Rate]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-card}
[11.9%]{.stat-value}
[Extreme Poverty]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-card}
[339]{.stat-value}
[Municipalities Mapped]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat-card}
[9.7%]{.stat-value}
[Child Stunting Rate]{.stat-label}
:::
:::

## Methodology at a Glance

::: {.pipeline-flow}
::: {.pipeline-box}
Survey Data<br/>(EH 2024)
:::
[&rarr;]{.pipeline-arrow}
::: {.pipeline-box}
Layer 1<br/>DRF
:::
[&rarr;]{.pipeline-arrow}
::: {.pipeline-box}
Layer 2<br/>BYM2
:::
[&rarr;]{.pipeline-arrow}
::: {.pipeline-box}
Layer 3<br/>OT Calibration
:::
[&rarr;]{.pipeline-arrow}
::: {.pipeline-box}
Municipal<br/>Estimates
:::
:::

Our three-layer approach combines the flexibility of machine learning (Distributional Random Forests) with the spatial coherence of Bayesian models (BYM2) and the distributional fidelity of Optimal Transport — producing estimates that are both locally precise and nationally consistent.

::: {.callout-highlight}
**Data sources**: Bolivia Household Survey (EH 2024, 12,718 households) and Census of Population and Housing (CPV 2024, ~3.5 million households). Nutrition estimates use the National Food and Nutrition Survey (ENSDA 2023, 19,059 children).
:::

## Explore the Results

| Section | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [Methodology](methodology.qmd) | Detailed description of the DRF + BYM2 + OT pipeline |
| [Poverty Maps](poverty.qmd) | Interactive maps of poverty headcount, gap, and severity |
| [Nutrition Maps](nutrition.qmd) | Stunting, wasting, and underweight at the municipal level |
| [Data & Downloads](data.qmd) | Downloadable estimates, shapefiles, and replication code |

## Citation

> Hernani-Limarino, W.L. (2025). *Small Area Estimation of Poverty and Nutrition in Bolivia Using Distributional Random Forests, Bayesian Spatial Smoothing, and Optimal Transport*. Working Paper.
